ABOUT YOU

Hybrid | Hamburg or Berlin, Germany | Experienced

* (Senior) Frontend Engineer - Webstack (all genders) – Hamburg or Berlin, Germany

Apply now

## About our Product & Tech Department

Our mission is to digitize the classic shopping stroll, turning it into a personalized, inspiring experience for every customer. Technology and data sit at the center of everything we build. We are not a fashion company with a tech team on the side. Tech drives our product, our strategy, and our growth.  
Our proprietary tech stack powers our own shops and SCAYLE, our commerce infrastructure solution that serves over 350 brands globally. Same foundation, bigger stage. The challenges keep things interesting.

## What To Expect

* Develop and maintain high-quality, performant, and scalable frontend features for our Web Shop (Desktop, Mobile Web, Tablet) using React and TypeScript.
* Collaborate closely with product managers, UX designers, and QA managers to translate concepts and requirements into user-friendly and reliable features.
* Take an active role in solving complex technical problems and architecting innovative, sustainable frontend solutions.
* Take ownership of large-scale projects from conception to deployment, requiring little oversight.
* Foster a culture of high-quality code, continuous learning, and knowledge sharing.
* Actively participate in code reviews to ensure code quality, maintainability, and adherence to best practices.
* Contribute to optimizing our frontend architecture, performance (Core Web Vitals), and development workflows.

## Who You Are

* At least 4 yearsexperience with Frontend Development.
* Expert proficiency in JavaScript and TypeScript.
* Deep experience with React and its ecosystem (e.g. hooks, context).
* Strong skills in HTML and modern CSS (e.g. CSS-in-JS, responsive design).
* Solid understanding of frontend testing frameworks (e.g. Jest, React Testing Library).
* Experience integrating with REST APIs and managing application state (e.g., Redux, Zustand, React Query).
* Familiarity with build tools (e.g. Webpack, Vite) and package managers (e.g. npm, yarn).
* Strong knowledge of web performance optimization principles and accessibility (a11y).
* Proficient with Git and comfortable with CI/CD workflows.
* Excellent problem-solving: You can solve conceptual, non-standard problems and exercise solid judgment based on analyzing multiple sources of information.
* High autonomy and ownership: You are motivated, take matters into your own hands, and can lead a complex project or process with little oversight from your manager.

Nice to have

* Experience with server-side rendering (SSR).
* Experience working in a large-scale e-commerce environment.
* Knowledge of Node.js.
* Experience building or maintaining a design system or component library.
* Experience with end-to-end testing tools like Cypress.

### Salary range

60k - 70

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth, 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

### About the role

The Product team shapes the experiences that power Revolut. They turn complex problems into simple, scalable solutions — collaborating across Design, Engineering, and Data to build features our customers love.

We’re looking for a Lead Applied AI Engineer who can manage their product roadmap, including day-to-day operations, project execution, and risk assessment.

From conception to launch and beyond, you’ll collaborate cross-functionally and communicate with senior management to be the go-to person for your product.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

* Owning the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for AI-powered solutions, leveraging LLMs and other machine learning technologies

title: Using AI in the interview process
content:
# Using AI in the interview process

At GitLab, we embrace AI to help our teams work better and faster while following our CREDIT values and AI Ethics Principles. Similar to how we integrate AI into our products to support customers throughout the software development lifecycle, we also encourage our teams, and candidates, to use AI thoughtfully to boost productivity and drive better outcomes.

## Our AI ethics principles in talent acquisition

We leverage AI powered technology that is tested to prevent bias.

We protect candidate information and ensure any AI powered technology meet GitLab's security standards.

We evaluate how AI can impact candidates and avoid implementations that could cause unintentional harm.

We carefully evaluate what data is used by AI, how it is used, and ensure comprehensive compliance with privacy commitments.

Our AI-assisted recruitment processes include meaningful human review, feedback mechanisms, and continuous improvement.

## How we view AI

We view AI as a powerful enhancement to human capabilities while recognizing that understanding potential, building relationships, and making fair hiring decisions remain fundamentally human activities. We do not leverage AI for decision making in our hiring process and recognize the importance of human-centric activities in our recruitment lifecycle.

In line with our AI Ethics Principles, our Talent Acquisition team uses AI in the following ways:

### Content creation

* **Job descriptions:** We may use AI to help draft job descriptions that are then reviewed and refined by our hiring teams.
* **Interview questions:** We may use AI to help generate relevant interview questions that align with the role requirements to provide efficient, structured interview processes.
* **Sourcing messages:** We may use AI to draft initial outreach messages that are then personalized by our recruiters.

### Process enhancement

* **Interview summaries:** With appropriate consent, we may use AI to help summarize interview feedback, reducing the burden on interviewers while ensuring comprehensive documentation.
* **Analytics:** We use AI to analyze recruitment metrics and identify opportunities for improvement.

### Human oversight

* **Human review:** All AI-generated content undergoes meaningful human review before use.
* **Decision-making:** Hiring decisions are always made by human team members.
* **Regular bias review:** We regularly review our AI policies in Talent Acquisition to ensure they test for potential bias.

## Our guiding principles for candidates

Learn how we use AI to enhance our interview process while keeping people at the centre of hiring decisions. We'll share our ethical guidelines, what to expect during interviews, and how you can transparently leverage AI as a candidate in the interview process.

## AI Use Guidelines By Stage

You may use AI to assist with your resume or cover letter, but the information must accurately represent your qualifications and skills.

You will play a key role in improving the quality, UX, and depth of our mobile application while contributing significantly to the technical foundations of mobile development for the team.

Working in the tech team at Doctolib involves building innovative products and features to improve the daily lives of care teams and patients. We work in feature teams in an agile environment, while collaborating with product, design, and business teams.

Your responsibilities include but are not limited to:

* Establish and evolve mobile architecture patterns and technical standards for the patient domain

* Our mobile applications are built with React Native. The rest of our stack is composed of Rails, TypeScript, Java, Python and Kotlin.
* We leverage AI ethically across our products to empower patients and health professionals. Discover our AI vision here and learn about our first AI hackathon here!

Welcome to Tallinn, where Pipedrive was born.

Come and join Pipedrive’s Tallinn team!

See open positions

## About us

Pipedrive was born in Tallinn. What started as a small startup in a shared office has grown into an international company with an entire building in Tallinn’s vibrant WoHo quarter. Today, Tallinn is home to our engineering and product hub, where 300+ team members from 30+ nationalities work together – many of them engineers.

Pipedrive is proud to be Estonia’s fifth unicorn and has consistently been recognized as one of the best places in the country to work since 2016, according to both the media and our employees. And we’re not just about jobs – at Pipedrive, you’ll have the chance to grow, learn and make a real impact by helping shape a product used by more than 100,000 companies worldwide.

## Our traditions

At Pipedrive, we love to celebrate the moments that matter – from birthdays and work anniversaries to promotions. One of our most beloved traditions is getting together for *Kringle*, a classic Estonian pastry, though you’ll also see pizza, donuts or pancakes make an appearance. To make sure no celebration goes unnoticed, we’ve got a kitchen-alert Slack channel to keep everyone in the loop.

Our biggest annual event is the Summer Garage – a full-day celebration that brings teams together to connect, unwind and enjoy live music performed by our very own band (and sometimes a guest artist or two).

We also host occasional hackathons and innovation accelerators to spark new ideas for improving the product and how we work together.

We believe in a balance of fun and focus. From Lightning Talks where anyone can practice their presentation skills with a topic they care about, to Sudoku tournaments and gaming challenges organized by teammates, there’s always something to get involved in.

We’re also proud to contribute to the wider community: Our team actively supports the local engineering, product and HR scenes in Estonia by hosting in-house meetups, participating in conferences and speaking at events.

Staying active outside of work is also a big part of our culture. Whether it's a friendly game of table tennis, a disc golf match or a padel session – one of the most popular sports among our Tallinn team – there's always fun going on.

## Where you’ll find us

You’ll find us in Tallinn’s modern WoHo district, where our custom-designed office makes it easy to feel at home. Each of our eight themed floors – park, nature, sports, IT, music, art, movies and culture – is fully immersive. Whether you’re jamming on the music floor’s instruments or exploring the art gallery, there’s something for everyone.

The heart of the office is our all-hands area, where people gather for a coffee or something else from our well stocked kitchens. # Tartu

Welcome to Tartu, a vibrant student city where everything is within walking distance.

Come and join Pipedrive’s Tartu team!

See open positions

## About us

Pipedrive’s Tartu office opened in 2015 and is now home to one of our four engineering hubs, along with Tallinn, Prague and Lisbon. With 60+ employees representing more than 10 nationalities, we’re a tight-knit team with big ideas and even bigger energy.

While Pipedrive has grown into Estonia’s fifth unicorn, the culture in Tartu still carries the spirit of our early startup days – where creativity, humor and collaboration lead the way.  
Sure, their jokes are hilarious, but don’t let that fool you: “Tartu delivers” is this office’s motto, for a reason. The winning teams at our hackathons often come from here and the quality of their work speaks for itself.

Over the years, we’ve been recognized with multiple best employer awards in Estonia, but what makes this office truly special is its people. Whether you're a seasoned engineer or just starting your career, you'll find the space and support you need to thrive here.

## Our traditions

We like to keep things down to earth and fun. Whether that’s sharing self-organized lunches, competing in a bake-off, or cheering each other on during a Lightning Talk (where employees get to hone their presentation skills on a topic of their choice), there’s always something happening.

Each month, we come together for different activities – from sauna nights, board game battles and gaming tournaments to hiking, biking and even winter swimming. We’ve got a strong gaming culture, a love for the outdoors and a great sense of humor that ties it all together.

We also host our own version of Pipehikes, inspired by our team in Lisbon, and open the office doors to our little ones during PipeKids – an annual afternoon of creativity and play for employees’ families.  
 And at the end of the year, we join Pipedrivers around the world for our winter holiday celebration: an in-house award show full of recognition, fun sketches and community spirit, dubbed “The Pipeys”.

Whether you're leading a talk, jumping into a tournament or just relaxing in the sauna, there’s a place for you here.

## Where you’ll find us

Our Tartu office is located near the city center in the city’s first LEED Gold-certified office building – a modern, energy-efficient tower that reflects our commitment to a thoughtful work environment. We occupy two cozy, creative floors, giving us plenty of natural light and sweeping views of the Emajõgi river.

One is inspired by video games – yes, we game a lot – while the other is themed around Estonian birds. If you’re curious, yes, we really do enjoy pronouncing names like Ööbik and Tuttpütt.  
 We’ve created a welcoming space where everyone feels at home. Our slipper policy says it all. And with regular visits from our beloved office dogs (including Juuni, who even has her own Slack emojis), the vibe is friendly and relaxed.

# Senior Software Engineer, Aiven AppsHelsinki, Uusimaa, Finland

Apply now

We’re a global team of over 400 people, working together to push the boundaries of open-source technology and multi-cloud solutions. Our vision is to help developers, builders, and creators bring their ideas to life with speed and simplicity, by providing a cloud data platform that makes open-source databases, search, streaming, and application infrastructure easily accessible to everyone.

### **The Role:**

We are hiring for a full-stack engineer to work on the recently launched Aiven application runtime: Aiven Apps, which enables users to build complete solutions in Aiven that encompass data and application runtime.

We are looking for product-minded engineers who have a keen interest in the business and user behavior, are proactive with product development ideas and tradeoffs, and want to understand the “Why?” behind all things. As a product-minded engineer you communicate well with engineers and non-engineers alike and are interested in how other functions work.

As we are looking for a senior engineer, we expect you to raise the bar for the team through technical mentorship. We’d love you to show us how we can improve what we do. Our teams are proud of what we build and value curiosity, healthy debate, and exchange of ideas.

### **What You’ll Do:**

Here are some example areas you may work on:

* Iterating on the Console UX for creating and managing applications
* Designing and implementing new RESTful APIs or extending existing ones.
* Owning the end-to-end implementation and maintenance of new features
* Making sure our technical foundation stays in shape by highlighting needed technical improvements.

### **What we’re looking for:**

Our frontend is built with TypeScript, React and Redux, and our backend centers on Python and PostgreSQL. As such, to succeed in the role you have:

* Experience leading the planning and implementation of an end-to-end feature
* Proficiency in TypeScript and experience in Python. Experience with Golang is considered a plus.
* Experience with relational databases and schema design
* Experience deploying and managing containers in a cloud environment
* Fluent communication skills in English, written and verbal

### **Global Benefits:**

Our global benefits are designed to help you thrive and grow, personally and professionally:

* Participate in Aiven’s equity plan.
* Balance work and life with our hybrid work policy.
* Choose the equipment you need to set yourself up for success.
* Use your Professional Development Plan budget for learning opportunities.
* Receive holistic wellbeing support through our global Employee Assistance Program.
* Inquire about our Global Time Off Commitment (Parental and Sick Leave, as well as Personal Time)
* Enjoy country-specific benefits for our global cast.
